stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: Waymo suspends freeway driving amid safety concerns
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo has suspended freeway driving across all US markets due to safety concerns.
Customers observed the service change when freeway routes were removed from trip projections.
This represents a significant operational rollback in autonomous vehicle deployment.

stories.title: Megalodon: Mass GitHub Repo Backdooring via CI Workflows
stories.author: safedep.io via hauleth
bullet_points: The story details a significant security vulnerability known as Megalodon.
The attack vector involves mass backdooring of GitHub repositories through CI/CD workflows.
This represents a critical threat to software supply chain security.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued relevance and activity surrounding Meta's Llama models in the AI community.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets and accelerates execution on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local, on-device operation.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI processes entirely on the user's own hardware for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: Meta’s Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching a new 'Forum' app to house Facebook Groups in a dedicated application.
The app integrates a dedicated AI chatbot, signaling a major push into conversational AI within Meta's ecosystem.
This move represents a strategic attempt by Meta to compete with platforms like Reddit and leverage AI features similar to Google's AI Overview.

stories.title: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business ‘fair and square’
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Google is appealing a federal ruling that declared it an illegal search monopolist.
Google's legal argument contends the ruling bypassed established legal standards.
The company asserts its market success is achieved 'fair and square' through competitive means.

stories.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's new AI Overviews feature is exhibiting significant functional bugs.
The AI is failing to provide expected search summaries, instead defaulting to chatbot-like responses.
This highlights current reliability and accuracy challenges in large-scale generative AI implementations.

stories.title: Trump abruptly cancels EO signing event after top AI firm CEOs declined to go
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Trump abruptly canceled an EO signing event related to AI safety testing.
The cancellation occurred after top AI firm CEOs declined to attend the event.
The delay suggests the administration views AI safety regulations as a potential 'innovation blocker'.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment.
Proposes 'MOSS' for self-evolution by enabling source-level rewriting within agent systems.
Highlights that existing self-evolving agents only modify text artifacts, leaving core agent code untouched.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to overcome latency bottlenecks in stateful AI agents.
Addresses the high computational cost of traditional checkpoint/rollback (C/R) mechanisms, which currently take hundreds of milliseconds to seconds.
Enables high-frequency state exploration necessary for advanced AI techniques like tree search and reinforcement learning by optimizing state management.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment, failing to learn from interactions.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a system enabling self-evolution by rewriting the agent's source code, not just configuration files.
Highlights that critical agent logic (routing, state invariants) resides in code, which previous self-evolution methods ignored.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates six major commercial AI chatbots (including Gemini, GPT, Claude, and Grok) on their ability to handle emerging news facts.
It addresses a critical gap in research regarding how proprietary AI systems synthesize and retrieve information from real-time news sources across different languages and regions.
The evaluation utilized 2,100 factual questions derived from same-day BBC News reporting over a two-week period.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating its primary function.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Marketer that claimed it could tap devices for ad targeting will pay $880K settlement
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: A marketing company faced a significant settlement after claiming its technology could tap devices for ad targeting.
The primary company agreed to pay an $880,000 settlement.
Two other marketing companies were also fined $25,000 each in relation to the issue.

stories.title: Soaring solar and a surge in hydro push more coal off the US grid
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Increasing solar and hydroelectric power generation is contributing to a reduction in coal usage on the US power grid.
Initial data from 2026 suggests that previous year's energy trends might have been anomalous.
The story addresses a significant, ongoing shift in the US energy mix driven by renewable adoption.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current LLMs, which often produce low-entropy responses when trained with a single scalar reward.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize better and support diverse reward functions during inference-time search (e.g., AlphaEvolve).
This research is relevant for improving LLMs' ability to perform robustly in novel environments requiring varied task-specific evaluations.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed, recurrent memory states without data corruption.
Improves efficiency by reducing sequence mixing to linear time and decoding to constant memory.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The paper introduces LCGuard, a mechanism designed to ensure safe sharing of latent communication within multi-agent LLM systems.
Latent communication via transformer Key-Value (KV) caches is noted as a method to enhance efficiency and information richness in LLM coordination.
The core problem addressed is the opacity and potential privacy/security risks associated with KV caches encoding sensitive agent-specific information.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze is a new framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
It specifically addresses two key challenges in eye-tracking analysis: handling missing data (like blinks) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
The technology has potential applications in safety-critical systems, such as driver vigilance monitoring and automated flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time cognitive load assessment on wearables.
Proposes CogAdapt, a framework designed to adapt pre-trained clinical ECG models to the specific constraints of wearable sensors.
Focuses on improving cross-subject generalization and overcoming sensor configuration mismatches in cognitive monitoring.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
Proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and handle diverse reward functions required by inference-time search methods (like AlphaEvolve).
Focuses on improving the diversity of LLM outputs, which is crucial for effective exploration and search in novel environments.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address multiple robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian accordingly.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent information (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It introduces 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to guard or sanitize the KV cache during inter-agent communication.
The work focuses on improving efficiency and information richness in multi-agent coordination while mitigating the risks of exposing sensitive agent states.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current log anomaly detectors which operate at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Highlights the technical challenge of message-level detection due to heterogeneous subsystems and ambiguous event templates.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit systematic and asymmetric political bias, termed 'covert political bias,' when handling opposing political viewpoints.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert political bias manifests in LLMs.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantify the symmetry of rhetoric and depth in LLM responses.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how the temporal ordering of data during pre-training affects Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs are trained on shuffled data, leading to poor temporal grounding.
Introduces a new benchmark containing over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a critical gap in biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information, which is vital for accurate clinical reasoning.
Existing KGs fail to capture when a clinical finding becomes relevant over a patient's lifespan, limiting their utility for longitudinal analysis.
The introduction of ChronoMedKG provides a new, temporally-grounded KG and benchmark specifically designed to improve clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in medicine.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: Investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect' (AMEL) on LLM judgments, where prior conversation history biases subsequent evaluations.
Tested across 75,898 API calls using 11 models from major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models).
Highlights a critical reliability concern for using LLMs as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation).

stories.title: U.S. researchers face new restrictions on publishing with foreign collaborators
stories.author: ceejayoz
bullet_points: U.S. researchers are encountering new limitations regarding collaboration with international partners.
The restrictions specifically impact the ability to publish research findings with foreign collaborators.
This signals potential shifts in international academic and research cooperation policies.

stories.title: FTC to Require Cox Media Group to Pay Nearly $1million to Settle Charges They Deceived Customers About “Active Listening” AI-Powered Marketing Service
stories.author: ftc.gov via simonw
bullet_points: The FTC has mandated that Cox Media Group pay nearly $1 million to settle charges.
The charges relate to the company deceiving customers regarding its 'Active Listening' AI-powered marketing service.
This highlights regulatory scrutiny over the transparency and claims made by AI-driven marketing technologies.

stories.title: Zero-Idle Local LLMs: Running Llama 3 in AWS Lambda Containers
stories.author: dhananjay_lakkawar
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of running large language models (LLMs) like Llama 3 in serverless environments.
Focuses on achieving 'zero-idle' performance for local LLMs within AWS Lambda containers.
Suggests a practical solution for deploying resource-intensive AI workloads in a cost-efficient, event-driven manner.

stories.title: Agentic AI in Travel: Why UCP Isn't Travel-Ready Yet — and What We Measured
stories.author: benjifisher
bullet_points: The article discusses the application of Agentic AI within the travel industry.
It specifically addresses the readiness of UCP (likely a specific technology or standard) for advanced AI integration in travel systems.
Amadeus, a major industry player handling billions of daily flight searches, is mentioned as a key context provider.

stories.title: I'm 16, and I Built an AI Tool That Audits Your Technical Debt Without Ever Touching code
stories.author: prithvi_cs_d4551846dc2be5
bullet_points: A young developer created a CLI tool to audit technical debt in code repositories.
The tool is designed to be provider-agnostic and operate within a sandbox environment.
It represents a practical application of AI/ML for automated software quality assessment.
